feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! È il modo standard di rispondere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! It's the standard way to answer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you always answer 'Pronto?'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per chiedere l'identità di chi chiama.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! To ask the identity of the caller.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask 'Chi parla?'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To ask for someone.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you use 'C'è...?'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per confermare la propria identità.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To confirm your identity.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say 'it's me'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Very polite to ask at the beginning.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask 'Disturbo?'.

feedback.correctItalian: Corretto! Per sapere se l'altro può parlare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! To find out if the other person can talk.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'Hai un minuto?' is used.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Se non puoi parlare subito.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! If you can't talk right away.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say 'I'll call you back'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To check availability.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask 'Can you talk?'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! If you have things to do.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say 'I'm busy'.

english: I wanted to ask you something.
choicesEnglish: I wanted to ask you something.
I wanted to tell you something.
I wanted to give you something.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un modo comune per iniziare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa 'chiederti' per fare una domanda.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A common way to start.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you use 'ask you' to pose a question.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! We'll talk later.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means postponing the call.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Se la linea è disturbata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! If the line is noisy.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the verb 'sentire' is used.

english: The sound is bad / I can't hear well.
choicesEnglish: The sound is bad / I can't hear well.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Quando la qualità audio è scarsa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce alla qualità della chiamata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! When the audio quality is poor.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the call quality.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Quando la comunicazione si interrompe.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si dice che 'cade' la linea.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! When the communication is cut off.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say the line 'drops'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Quando il segnale è debole.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa 'campo' per il segnale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! When the signal is weak.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you use 'campo' for signal.

choicesEnglish: Do you want to talk now?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Se non senti bene l'altro.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si chiede di parlare 'più forte'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! If you can't hear the other person well.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask to speak 'louder'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Fondamentale se sospetti problemi tecnici.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Essential if you suspect technical issues.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say 'mi senti?'.

english: I wanted to tell you that...
choicesEnglish: I wanted to tell you that...

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To give information.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you use 'tell you' to inform.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un modo informale per dire 'ti ascolto'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! An informal way to say 'I'm listening'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a typical expression: 'dimmi tutto'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! Very polite.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask for 'a moment'.

english: Can you call me back in ten minutes?
choicesEnglish: Can you call me back in ten minutes?
Shall I call you back in ten minutes?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Se sei impegnato al momento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! If you are busy at the moment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask the other person to call back.

english: My phone is dying / battery is low.
choicesEnglish: My phone is dying / battery is low.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per avvisare che la chiamata potrebbe finire.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To warn that the call might end.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'scarico' refers to the battery.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Magari per la batteria finita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Maybe because the battery ran out.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the verb 'spegnersi' (to turn off) is used.

english: Sorry, I have the wrong number.
choicesEnglish: Sorry, I have the wrong number.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Very common.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say 'I have the wrong number'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! La risposta cortese a un errore.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa 'si figuri' in contesti formali.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The polite response to a mistake.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'si figuri' is used in formal contexts.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! In segreteria o a una persona.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! In voicemail or to a person.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the verb 'lasciare' (to leave) is used.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Very modern.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means sending a message.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Very useful for meeting up.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask for the GPS 'position'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una frase tipica quando sei in ritardo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che mancano pochi minuti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! A typical phrase when you are late.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means only a few minutes are left.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To give an instruction.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say 'aspettami'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To set the time.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask 'At what time...?'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa 'facciamo' per decidere insieme.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! To propose a time.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'facciamo' is used to decide together.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To confirm with enthusiasm.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you agree.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'non penso che sia così'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To express doubt.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I don't think that's the case'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un modo per confermare di aver ricevuto l'info.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! A very natural reaction.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a way to confirm you've received the info.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si chiede se l'altro ne è certo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To ask for confirmation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask if the other is certain.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Very reassuring.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'stay calm'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si chiede se va tutto bene.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! A very common greeting.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you ask if everything is fine.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la risposta a 'Tutto a posto?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The standard answer.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the answer to 'Tutto a posto?'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per restare in attesa di notizie.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To stay tuned for news.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'give me updates'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Quando non hai ancora una risposta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! When you don't have an answer yet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you postpone the information.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per augurarsi qualcosa di positivo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa per essere ottimisti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To wish for something positive.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's used to be optimistic.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si dice quando qualcosa va male.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To express regret.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's said when something goes wrong.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa per il sollievo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Expresses relief.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's used for relief.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa per chiedere conferma.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Reaction of surprise.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's used to ask for confirmation.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per forte sorpresa o incredulità.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! For strong surprise or disbelief.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'it's incredible'.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! A very natural exclamation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's an expression of amazement.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per chiudere un discorso senza successo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To close a discussion without success.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'it's nothing'.

english: I have to let you go / I have to hang up.
choicesEnglish: I have to let you go / I have to hang up.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il modo per dire che devi riagganciare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To end the call.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the way to say you have to hang up.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Molto comune per salutarsi al telefono.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Very common for saying goodbye on the phone.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the verb 'sentirsi' (to hear from each other) is used.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A generic greeting.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'see you in a bit'.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un modo tipico per salutare cari e amici.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Very affectionate.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a typical way to greet loved ones and friends.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Very friendly.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a 'strong' hug.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per chiudere la chiamata in modo informale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To end the call informally.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you repeat 'ciao' at the end.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A wish for good health.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'take care of yourself'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per estendere i saluti alla famiglia o agli amici.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un invito a dare saluti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! To extend greetings to family or friends.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's an invitation to give greetings.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un saluto finale molto comune.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica un contatto futuro imminente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A very common final greeting.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates imminent future contact.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To thank the other person.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're thanking for the phone call.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exact! Returning the thanks.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you return the thank you.

english: Have a good rest of your day / evening (formal).
choicesEnglish: Have a good rest of your day / evening (formal).

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa per augurare buon lavoro o giornata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Very formal and elegant.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's used to wish a good continuation of work or day.
